topic: Telluric ~12 Hz peak — verification of ISS-1989 grammar patch + symmetry with WIN-008
our_argument_summary: WIN-009 stands as 'Refuted by Data'. The 12 Hz region is an inter-harmonic Schumann minimum (between 7.83 Hz fundamental and 14.3 Hz first harmonic), not a peak. The 14.3 Hz harmonic falsifies any resonance ceiling at 11.7 Hz. Independently fails the post-hoc test: the 'confirming' value comes from indexed published MT/ELF literature that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and the dome's monitor.py hardcodes both predicted and observed values rather than fetching live data — so the temporal-ordering condition required by the dome author's own definition of 'prediction' cannot be checked at all.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 16 of monitor.py (the WIN-009 entry) hardcodes predicted ≈ 11.788 Hz and observed ≈ 11.787 Hz — both values baked into the script. There is no API call, no live MT/ELF data fetch, and no comparison against any independently-sourced observation. pass=True is the literal output of comparing two strings of constants.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded. The 'check' is a pred==obs comparison where both sides are constants in the source file.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None visible — there's no tolerance to widen because there's no live data; both values are static.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. WIN-008's detail_evidence already lays out the monitor.py hardcoding in detail (Domain 16 predicted=11.788, observed=11.7869, dome formula f=c/(2D) only reproducing 11.787 Hz when D ≈ Earth's polar diameter). WIN-009 inherits this code-analysis context via its 'See WIN-008 for full analysis' cross-link rather than restating it; that factoring is defensible since the underlying claim shares the same code path.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Both predicted and observed values are static constants in monitor.py Domain 16; no fetch logic exists for this domain. Verified against WIN-008's c-cycle code analysis which examines the same Domain 16 entry.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Inter-harmonic spectral minima between Schumann modes are a textbook prediction of standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics (Chave & Jones 2012). The dome model takes this exact predicted minimum and relabels it as a positive resonance peak — same numerical region, same physics, opposite interpretation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann harmonic spectra (including the inter-harmonic minimum at ~11–12 Hz) have been characterized in published MT/ELF literature for decades. The dome monitor commit hardcoded the value rather than measuring it, and we could not locate any independent timestamp anchoring this prediction prior to data adoption. Same post-hoc structure as WIN-008.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's f = c/(2D) formula reproduces ≈11.787 Hz only when D ≈ Earth's polar diameter (12,713.6 km), NOT when D = the dome's firmament height (9,086 km → 16.50 Hz), disc radius (20,015 km → 7.49 Hz), or 2× firmament height (18,172 km → 8.25 Hz). The derivation silently substitutes a globe parameter to hit the target. WIN-008's detail_evidence carries the full numerical comparison.

holes_found.description: Verification: ISS-1989 grammar patch landed cleanly. The c4 major hole — 'a field-specific published dataset published MT and ELF spectral literature' — is now correctly written as 'a field-specific published dataset (published MT and ELF spectral literature) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit'. Parentheses restored as recommended in c4. The phrasing now matches WIN-008's post-hoc paragraph at the dataset-name slot, the legitimate-novelty carve-out clause, and the reference to 'indexed public datasets' — symmetry across the post-hoc-paragraph WIN family is restored.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Recorded for audit trail.

holes_found.description: WIN-009-specific divergence from WIN-008's post-hoc clause is intentional and correct. WIN-008 says 'the monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line, so the temporal ordering the definition requires cannot be checked from the repository history.' WIN-009 says 'the monitor script hardcodes both the predicted and the observed values — there is no independent data-fetch step, so the temporal ordering the definition requires cannot be checked at all.' The divergence is not a symmetry break — it reflects the actual factual difference between the two domains (WIN-008's monitor.py Domain has a data-fetch line whose timestamp could in principle be examined; WIN-009's Domain has no fetch step at all). c3 hole #0 was the original push to fix WIN-009's wording away from WIN-008's; that fix has held through c4 and remains correct.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Confirms c3 fix is durable.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c4 hole #1 — quantitative harmonic line still not in detail_evidence. ISS-1993 ('add quantitative harmonic line for standalone readability') is open and routed to analyst (status: assigned-analyst, severity: minor, class_hint: verification). Decider's routing decision is correct — this is an analyst-prose addition, not a curmudgeon-recommended patch. WIN-009's detail_evidence currently relies on the WIN-008 cross-link to carry the full harmonic spectrum (7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz). For a reader who lands on WIN-009 directly without clicking through, the 14.3 Hz harmonic IS named in the same paragraph ('robust first harmonic at ~14.3 Hz directly contradicts any resonance ceiling below that frequency'), so the core argument survives even without the full harmonic enumeration. The standalone-readability improvement remains a nice-to-have, not a defect.
holes_found.recommendation: Wait for ISS-1993 analyst response. No new action this cycle.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'ceiling below that frequency' phrasing in detail_evidence ('any resonance ceiling below that frequency') is technically correct but parses awkwardly. WIN-008's verdict text uses the cleaner 'directly falsifies any cutoff at 11.7 Hz'. A future polish pass could mirror the cleaner phrasing in WIN-009's detail_evidence ('directly contradicts any resonance ceiling at or below 14.3 Hz, including the dome\'s claimed 11.7 Hz peak'). NOT a defect — the current text is correct and a dome defender cannot exploit it. Logged as a stylistic improvement only; do not open an issue.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author is right that something interesting happens in the 11–12 Hz band of the Earth–ionosphere cavity spectrum (data/wins.json:WIN-009.detail_evidence cites the inter-harmonic structure between 7.83 and 14.3 Hz). The frequency region IS spectrally distinctive — it sits at a power minimum where the cavity's standing-wave structure produces less coherent energy than at the resonant modes on either side. A naive ELF spectrogram would absolutely show 'something' near 12 Hz, namely the gap between two resonance peaks.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right that the 11–12 Hz region is spectrally distinctive does not save the claim that it is a 'prominent peak' — the dome model has the sign inverted. A power minimum (data/wins.json:WIN-008.detail_evidence: 'inter-harmonic minimum the dome identifies is itself a prediction of standard spherical-cavity physics') is the opposite of a resonance peak. The dome relabels a globe-cavity null as a dome-cavity peak, then claims the relabel as a confirmed prediction. The kernel of truth (something is happening at 11-12 Hz) survives intact under standard physics; the dome's specific claim (it is a peak attributable to dome geometry) does not.

deeper_analysis:
ISS-1989 fix verification was the explicit purpose of this queue push (queue_id 336, class verification). The c4 review identified a single major grammar bug (concatenated noun phrases without connector) introduced by the earlier ISS-1867 patch. Decider self-applied the fix at 2026-05-12T09:19. Reading the current detail_verdict_text, the parentheses around '(published MT and ELF spectral literature)' are present and correctly placed; the full clause now reads naturally and matches WIN-008's parallel construction. WIN-008/WIN-009 post-hoc-paragraph symmetry is restored at all three matching slots: dataset-name parenthetical, legitimate-novelty carve-out, indexed-public-datasets reference. The single intentional divergence (data-fetch line wording) remains correct — WIN-009's 'hardcodes both the predicted and the observed values — there is no independent data-fetch step' accurately describes its monitor.py Domain (no fetch logic), distinct from WIN-008's situation (a fetch line with a same-commit hardcoded target).

No new substantive findings this cycle. Carry-over hole from c4 #1 (harmonic spectrum line) is properly routed to analyst as ISS-1993 (verification class hint) — that pathway should resolve in normal cadence. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' continues to hold; the scientific argument and the post-hoc finding are both intact.

Class-verification batching note: this review is item 1 of a possible 2-3-item batch run (queue items 336/337 are both class verification, both pushed at the same timestamp by the same decider run). Proceeding to triage queue_id 337 (WIN-017) for batchability.

citations_verified: doi.org/10.1017/CBO9781139020138 (Chave & Jones 2012, The Magnetotelluric Method, Cambridge — same citation as WIN-008, verified in earlier cycles)
citations_unchecked: john09289.github.io/predictions (dome author's site — link target referenced for 'dome author's own definition' of prediction; not re-verified this cycle)

No new substantive holes. Carry-over from c4 hole #1 (harmonic enumeration in detail_evidence) is already tracked as ISS-1993 (assigned-analyst), no new ISS needed. Recommended action: no_change.
